winner: I tried using my laptop to spray paint, but I kept getting pop-ups saying "Do you want to allow this app to make changes?" I guess it was trying to tell me I wasn't doing it right—it's not like I'm Spraying Excel with a can of Glossy!

candidates.text: I tried using my laptop to spray paint, but I kept getting pop-ups saying "Do you want to allow this app to make changes?" I guess it was trying to tell me I wasn't doing it right—it's not like I'm Spraying Excel with a can of Glossy!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative twist on common tech frustrations, clever use of required words, and a surprising punchline."}

candidates.text: I had to spray my laptop with Windex because it was running so slow, but I realized too late that I’d just made a big mess of my keyboard.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific, with a clear setup and punchline that plays on the unexpected consequence of using Windex on a laptop."}

candidates.text: I was trying to clean my laptop with spray paint when I realized, oh no—it's not cleaning me, it's making me cleaner!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke uses the required words and has a mild twist, but the humor is somewhat forced and the punchline lacks clarity and surprise."}

candidates.text: I was using my laptop to edit photos when I accidentally sprayed my keyboard with hairspray—turns out, it’s actually a good way to make the keys extra slippery for typing.
I got my laptop sprayed with Mace at a coffee shop today, and now every time I open it, Windows Defender thinks I'm trying to hack into a bank vault.
